def: s.m. Time; term; fixed time (for); season; hour; — duration; juncture; — opportunity; — (met.) adversity, distress
more: n. m. pl. auqāt . See belā , (1). 1. Time; term. 1. Vaqt kī khūbī hai . 2. Vaqt vaqt kī rāgni hai . Prov. Every rāgnī (song) has its appropriate hour and season. 3. Vaqt ko ganīmar samjho! Prov. Consider the present time sufficient. (Make the best of your time). 4. Donoṅ vaqt mile nahīṅ sīte, Sūraj kī āṅkh phūṭ jāëgī! Wom. One should not sew when day meets night, the eye of day would be put out! 5. Mehr-bān ho-ke bulā lo mujhe chāho jis vaqt, Maiṅ gayā-vaqt nahīṅ hūṅ ki phir ā bhī na sakūṅ . Gālib. To call me back whene'er you wish do you but deign, I'm not as time that's past which cannot come again. 2. Duration. 3. Opportunity. 4. Adversity. Vaqt to paigambaroṅ par bhī paṛtā hai. vaqt be-mauqah, vaqt nā-sāz . An unseasonable juncture. vaqt be-vaqt . 1. In season and out of season. 2. At all times. 3. Sometimes. vaqt par , adv. ety: prob. Prk. वालो and वालओ; S. पालः and पालकः; but often confounded with the primary suff. hārā and wālā in ani-hārā or ane-wālā = Ap. Prk. अणिअअडं or अणिअअडअ (with euphonic h or w inserted) = S. अनीय+क+रं, or र+कं
def: suff. (f. -ī), added to subst. to derive nouns implying possession or relation generally; — e.g. go-wāl, or go-wālā, s.m. cow-keeper, cow-herd (fr. go, 'cow'); — ghaṭ-wāl, s.m. wharf-keeper, wharfinger (fr. ghāṭ, 'landing-place'); — nāʼo-wālā, s.m. boat-keeper, boatman (fr. nāʼo, 'boat'); — ghar-wālā, s.m. master or owner of the house (fr. ghar, 'house'); — kapṛā-wālā, s.m. cloth-man, cloth-merchant (fr. kapṛā, 'cloth'); — roṭī-wālā, s.m. bread-man, baker (fr. roṭī, 'bread'); — dillī-wālā, belonging to, or a native of, Delhi; — it is even suffixed to foreign words, e.g. bakas-wālā, box-man, pedlar (fr. bakas, 'a box'); — it is sometimes used to form adj. fr. subst., e.g. kifāyat-wālā, 'economical' (fr. kifāyat, 'economy'); — gunjāʼish-wālā, 'capacious, spacious, roomy' (fr. gunjāʼish, 'capacity')
subs: (a)ne-wālā (or ani-hārā) is added to the roots of verbs to form fut. act. part., or nouns of agency, e.g. paṛh-ne-wālā, 'one who is going (or is about) to read'; 'a reader'; — de-ne-wālā, 'who is about to give'; 'a giver'; — ho-ne-wālā, 'that is about to happen'; 'that has yet to come to pass'; 'which is yet possible.'

more: n. f. 1. Guardianship. 2. A region; continent. 3. A foreign country, as England, China, etc. 4. Prophecy; the office of a saint. valāyat pānā . To get the rank of a saint. valāyat-zā , European; English. valāyat kā istehqāq . Right of guardianship. valāyat kī ḍāk . The English mail. sāhib-i-valāyat . A saint.
s. f. 1. An inhabited country, dominion, district; a foreign country, abroad. 2. Possession, being master of any thing, sovereignty. 3. Friendship, union (especially, with God); sanctity.
subs: To attain to the rank or dignity of a saint
adj. Born or produced in England or Europe, &c.; English; European
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian وِلَایَت (wilāyat), from Arabic وِلَايَة (wilāya). Compare Marathi विलायत (vilāyat), Bengali বিলাত (bilat) / বিলেত (bilet).

ety: for A. ولايًة, acc. of walāyat
def: adv. In behalf of; as the guardian of.
conj., contrac. of walek or wa-lekin, q.v.
conj. But, yet, however, for all that.
conj. But, yet, however, for all that.
s.f. Bridle, rein; bit of a bridle (i.q. bāg, q.v.). s.m. Garden, orchard, grove, cluster of trees, plantation (syn. bāṛī)
subs: s.f. Ornamental trees and flowers of talc, &c. carried in wedding processions ( = ārāʼish); (met.) Children, family, offspring
adj. Rejoicing, delighted; — bāg̠-bāg̠ honā or ho-jānā, v.n. To be delighted, to rejoice exceedingly
(and sabz bāg̠ dikhānā), To excite desire and expectation by deceitful promises (lit. 'to exhibit a blooming garden, as by legerdemain'); to delude, deceive, cheat
s.f. Gardening, garden cultivation, business of a gardener
s.m. Owner of a garden; gardener ( = bāg̠-bān).

def: s.m. = bali, q.v. s.f. Female child, &c. = bāl and bālā, q.q.v. s.f. Female child, girl, &c. (see bālā); — ear or spike of corn
more: n. m. valīah , n. f. 1. Lord; prince. 2. A guardian (of a ward). Jab tak aurat kā valī ijāzat na de nikāh nahīṅ hotā . A woman cannot marry without the consent of her guardian. 3. A saint; a holy man. Valī ke ghar shaitān . Prov. In the saint's abode the devil dwells. valī aslī , n. A natural guardian. valī allah . 1. God's people. 2. ( Ironic .) A knave.

s. m. 1. A prince, lord, master, friend, friend of God, saint. 2. A servant, a slave. ولي عہد walī-aﻌhd, 1. An heir. 2. A locum tenens, vicegerent. ولي عہدي walī-aﻌhd-ī, 1. Heritage, the state of being an heir apparent. 2. Deputyship. ولي کهنگر walī-khangar, s. m. An assistant, protector. ولي نعمت walī-niﻌmat, A title of respect by which a father is addressed, patron, benefactor.
subs: s.m. A person employed to watch ripe crops; the platform on which such a watchman sits; — (also = ) bāl-rakhāʼī, s.f. Price paid for watching of ripe crops.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ولی (walī), from Arabic وَلِيّ (waliyy).

ety: v.n. of the measure فَعِيل fr. ولي 'to be master of,' &c.
def: s.m. A master, lord, prince, governor; guardian (of a ward, &c.); helper, defender; — a friend, a favourite (of God, or a king); a saint (i.q. walī-ě-ḵẖudā); — a servant, slave
subs: s.m. 'Favourite of God'; God's people; — (ironic.) a knave
s.m. 'Friend or favourite of God,' a holy man, saint
s.m. 'Successor by virtue of a covenant,' destined or acknowledged successor, heir-apparent; — a vicegerent, locum tenens
v.t. To nominate or declare (one) a successor or heir (to a throne)
s.f. The status of an heir-apparent; heritage; — vicegerency, deputyship
s.m. An assistant, a protector; — (ironic.) one's betters
s.m. 'Lord of beneficence,' a benefactor, lord, master, patron; a title of respect by which a father is addressed.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ولی (walī), from Arabic وَلِيّ (waliyy).

def: suff. fem. of wālā, q.v.
more: n. m. ولي being near. 1. A guardian. 2. Owner; proprietor. Vālī sab kā Allah, ham to rakhvālī haiṅ . God owns all here below, me but a keeper know! 3. A ruler; chief; governor. vālī vāris . 1. Guardians. 2. Parents; patrons. be-vālī-vāris , adj. Without a guardian or protector; unprotected; unclaimed.
fem. of والا wālā, q. v.
(part. act. of ولي) s. m. A prince, a chief, superiour, sovereign, master, ruler.

ety: for A. والٍ wāliṉ, act. part. of ولي 'to be near,' &c.
def: s.m. A prince, ruler, governor; chief magistrate; — a guardian; — master, owner, proprietor
subs: Without guardian or protector, unprotected; — without owner or claimant, unclaimed.

def: n. m. An heir apparent. valī-ahd farzī , n. The heir presumptive. valī-ahdī , n. f. The status of an heir-apparent. valī/?/khaṅgar , n. m. ( Ironic .) One's betters. valī qarīb . A near guardian. valī-nemat , n. m. A title of respect, signifying a father, patron, benefactor.
s.m. Father
more: n. m. ولد offspring. Father. vālid mājid, vālid-i-buzurgvār . Respected father.
(part. act. of ولد Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. A father.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian وَالِد (wālid), from Arabic وَالِد (wālid).

ety: for A. والدة wālidat, fem. of wālid
def: s.f. Mother.
more: (part. act. fem. of ولد) s. f. A mother.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian والده (wālida), from Arabic وَالِدَة (wālida).

def: (obl. dual. of wālid), s.m. dual, Parents.
more: (dual of والد) s. m. plur. Parents.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian وَالِدَیْن (wālidayn), itself from Arabic وَالِدَيْن (wālidayn).

ety: act. part. of وله 'to be frightened,' &c.
def: adj. Astonished, stupefied, confounded; dispirited, driven to despair, distracted (with terror or grief); mad with love.
more: (from وله Heb. ) adj. Distracted, mad with love, stupified, astonished.

def: s.m. Feast, banquet; marriage-feast.
more: Mah. n. m. A marriage feast.

def: a female muslim saint.
wety: Feminine of ولی (valī).

def: s.m. Bark (of a tree); — a cloth or garment made of bark (worn by ascetics, &c.).

adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Beloved, desired, dear; — amiable; — supreme; superintending; — a lover; husband; favourite; friend; — a superintendent, overseer, master (see ballabh); — a chief herdsman; — a horse with good marks.
s.f. A beloved female; a mistress; a wife.

r: waʼllāh, waʼl-lāh aʻlam, waʼl-lāh aʻlam biʼṣ-ṣawāb, waʼl-lāh biʼl-lāh
def: And God; — (in swearing) by God
more: intj. By God! Heaven is my witness! vallah o ālam, vallah ālam bis-savāb ; H. Rām jāne , God knows.
subs: God knoweth; God is most wise
God knoweth the truth or what is right
By God (I swear); God is my witness.

def: s.m. Earth thrown up by white ants, moles, &c.; ant-hill; mole-hill; — the white ant (?); — the poet Vālmīki (author of the Rāmāyaṇ).
s.m. Name of the celebrated author of the Rāmāyaṇ.
s.m. = next, q.v.
= H ولتي वलती waltī, s.f. dialec. forms of اولتي oltī, q.v.
more: s. f. (dakh. i. q. کناره سايبان) Hem or border of a canopy? eaves of a house.

def: = H ولتهي वलथी walthī, s.f. dialec. forms of اولتي oltī, q.v.
more: s. f. (dakh. i. q. کناره سايبان) Hem or border of a canopy? eaves of a house.

def: s.m. = ولوله walwala, q.v.
more: (from ولول Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. Howling, lamenting. 2. A tumult, noise, uproar, murmur.

ety: for A. ولولة walwalat, inf. n. of the quadr. v. وَلْوَلَ 'to howl,' &c.
def: s.m. Howling, lamenting, wailing, crying; — wail, lamentation; — confused murmur, noise, tumult, uproar.
more: n. m. 1. 1. A gush of passion; ardour; fervour. The jab talak adheṛ rahe tau bhī valvale, Aur jab sufaid ho-ke hue barf ke ḍale . Nazīr. 2. Pining; repining. valvalāt' , A. H. n. f. Howling; wailing. valvalā'nā , A. H., v. n. 1. To lament; wail. 2. See balbalānā , 2, 3.
(from ولول Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. Howling, lamenting. 2. A tumult, noise, uproar, murmur.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic وَلْوَلَة (walwala).

ety: walwala, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि (caus. aug.) + nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To howl; wail, lament; — to low or bubble (as a camel, esp. when in rut), &c. (i.q. balbalānā, q.v.).

ety: walwalā(nā) + āṭ = āhaṭ = āwaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअट्टं = S. इतव्य+क+त्वं
def: s.f. Howling, wailing, lamentation.
s.m. Boyhood, childhood, infancy, youth
subs: (˚ya+av˚), s.f., or wālya-daśā, s.f., or wālya-kāl, s.m. The age, or period, or condition, of childhood or infancy.

def: s.m. Debt; loan; — credit; — lending; — borrowing
more: s. m. 1. Debt, credit, lending, borrowing. 2. (for فام) Colour. وام دار wām-dār, Debtor or creditor.

def: adj. (used in comp.) = فام fām, q.v. ( = wām, pām; prob. akin to S. उपमः), postpn. Like, resembling; (aff. to nouns denoting colour) of the colour of, approaching to the colour of (e.g. siyāh-fām, 'blackish'; laʻl-fām, 'ruby-coloured').
intj. = بم bam, q.v. adj. & s.m. Left, not right ( = bāṅwāṅ); adverse, &c. (see vāma); — the left side; an enemy
s.f. A woman; — a name of Gaurī, and of Lakshmī, and of Sarasvatī.

adj. Reverse; adverse, opposite, contrary; — left (not right); — crooked; — vile, base, wicked, bad; — short; — beautiful, handsome, pleasing; — s.m. An epithet of Śiva, and of Kām-dev (the god of love); — a breast, an udder; — an animal; a sentient being; — a snake
subs: (˚ma+āć˚), s.m. The left-hand ritual or doctrine of the Tantras, i.e. the worship of the Śakti or Female Energy personified as the wife of Śiva (according to the grosser system, in which the eating of flesh, drinking of spirits, &c. is practised); unsound doctrine, heterodoxy
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Following, or a follower of, the left-hand ritual of the Tantras; — heterodox; — a heterodox person
s.m. The reverse side; — the left side; — good fortune or share
s.f. An epithet of any beautiful woman
s.m. A name of Śiva; — a proper name
s.m. The left-hand way or ritual (i.q. vāmāćār, q.v.)

def: s.m. The act of vomiting, &c. (i.q. baman, q.v.)

adj. & s.m. Dwarfish, short in stature; depressed; — low, vile, base; — a dwarf; — Vishṇu in his dwarf-incarnation (the fifth of the avatārs of Vishṇu, undertaken to humble the pride of the Daitya Bali, who had acquired dominion over the three worlds); — the elephant that supports the south quarter
subs: s.m. One of the Hindū purāṇas, containing an account of the dwarf-incarnation of Vishṇu.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit वामन (vāmaná). Doublet of بَونَا (baunā).

def: s.f. Name of an Apsaras.
part. Tired, fatigued; — remaining or loitering behind; — unfolded, open, exposed (to view).
s.m. pl. (of wā-mānda, q.v.), Those who are tired; — those who remain behind.
s.f. The remaining or lagging behind (esp. from fatigue); — openness; exposure.
more: s. f. The remaining behind (especially, through weariness).

def: s.f. A female dwarf.
s.f. A leech (syn. joṅk).
s.f. = S وامتو वामत्व vāmatva, s.m. Contrariety, perverseness; — wickedness; craftiness; — disfavour.
s.m. = S وامتا वामता vāmatā, s.f. Contrariety, perverseness; — wickedness; craftiness; — disfavour.
s.f. A mare; — a she-ass; — a young female elephant; — a female jackal.
adj. In debt (syn. qarẓ-dār); — distressed, unfortunate.
s.f. Vomiting, sickness, nausea, qualmishness; vomit; — an emetic
subs: s.m. A name of fire; — a rogue, cheat.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Vomiting, being sick; inclined to vomit; — one who vomits, or who is sick at stomach.
adj. & s.m. Loving, enamoured (of); — a lover; — name of the famous lover of ʻAẕrā.
more: 1. part. act. (of ومق) A lover. 2. n. prop. Name of a most famous lover of عذرا aﻌẕrā.

def: adv. (dialec.) = وهان wahāṅ, q.v.
more: ( In Comp .) Possessing; endowed with, as dhan-vān possessing wealth.
(particle in compos.) Possessing, endowed with, &c. as, دهن وان धनवान् dhan-wān, Possessing wealth, rich.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit -वान् (-vān), masculine nominative singular of -वत् (-vat).

ety: Ap. Prk. वँओ,fem. विँआ; Prk. मओ, fem. मिआ; S. म+कः, fem. म+इका
def: suff. (f. wīṅ), added to cardinal numbers (after the fourth) to form the ordinals; e.g. pāṅć-wāṅ (f. -wīṅ), the fifth; — bārah-wāṅ, the twelfth
subs: the same form repeated has a distributive signification
e.g. das-wāṅ-das-wāṅ, Every tenth; by tens; ten to each.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit -वान् (-vān), masculine nominative singular of -वत् (-vat).

def: suff. = بان bān, q.v. s.f. Quality, property, temper, disposition, nature, character, conduct, manners, habit, custom (cf. ān-bān, s.v. ān); — s.m. The marriage rite which prescribes a certain number of baths (generally from three to eleven) for the bride and bridegroom
subs: v.n. To take the baths prescribed by the marriage rite (mentioned above)
v.n. To fall into, or to acquire or form, a habit, &c.; to be trained; to be accustomed (to)
v.n. To lay the foundation of character, &c., cultivate a habit, &c.; to educate, train; to habituate, accustom, inure; to naturalize
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit -वान् (-vān), masculine nominative singular of -वत् (-vat).

def: s.m. Blowing; — living; going, moving; rolling; surging; a flood; a heavy sea; — the high wave in Indian rivers (com. called 'the bore;' see bān); — a perfume, fragrance.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit -वान् (-vān), masculine nominative singular of -वत् (-vat).

def: s.m. A mat of straw; — a hole in the wall of a house.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit -वान् (-vān), masculine nominative singular of -वत् (-vat).

def: s.m. A shaft, an arrow, a dart (i.q. bān, q.v.); — the feathered part of an arrow; — a symbolical expression for the number five (from the five arrows of Kām-dev); — a kind of reed or cane; a pipe, flute; — fire; — a kind of rocket (used in battle), a sky rocket, a grenade; — udder (of a cow); — name of an Asur (a son of Rājā Bali, an enemy of Vishṇu, and favourite of Śiva)
subs: (˚na+as˚), s.m. The Asur or demon Vāṇ
s.f. The bed or couch of arrows (a peculiar method of transfixing each other with arrows practised by the gods in their mutual strifes)
part. adj. (f. -ī), Slain by an arrow or a dart
s.f. The wife of the Asur Vān.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit -वान् (-vān), masculine nominative singular of -वत् (-vat).

def: s.m. A forest, jungle, wood, &c. (i.q. ban, q.v.); — water; a fountain, spring; a cascade; — dwelling in a forest; — residence abroad; — dwelling-place, abode, residence, house
subs: (na˚+āś˚), s.m. Abode in the forest, the third āśram or stage in a Brāhmaṇ's life, when he becomes a Vāna-prasth or hermit
(˚na+āś), adj. & s.m. Living in a forest; — an inhabitant of a forest or wood
(˚na+an˚), s.m. The skirts of a wood; neighbourhood of a forest
(˚na+an˚), s.m. The middle or interior of a wood; — another wood
or van-prasthāyī, adj. & s.m. = ban-prasth, q.v.
(f. -ikā), or van-ćārī (f. -iṇī), or van-ćar (f. -ī), adj. & s.m. = ban-ćar, q.v.
s.m. 'Forest-king,' an epithet of the lion
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Being or abiding in a forest; — a hermit, an anchorite (syn. van-vāsī)
part. adj. (f. -ā), Situate or being in a forest; sylvan; wild; savage
s.m. A species of arum, A. colocasia
s.m. Honey produced in the jungle, wild honey
(f. -ā), or van-vāsī (f. -inī), adj. & s.m. = ban-bās, or ban-bāsī, q.v.
vulg. ban-bāsī, s.f. Dwelling in the forest; — the state of one who dwells in the forest; the life of a hermit
(vane, S. loc. of vana), adj. & s.m. = van-ćar or ban-ćar, q.v. (For other compounds see ban.)

def: suff. (f. -vatī), forming adjectives of possession from substantives; e.g. dhan-wān, 'possessing wealth, wealthy,' fr. dhan, 'wealth.'
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit -वान् (-vān), masculine nominative singular of -वत् (-vat).

def: adj. Belonging or relating to a wood or forest; — relating to a dwelling in a wood or to a house, &c.; — s.m. A number of woods, a collection of groves or thickets
subs: vulg. bān-prasth, s.m. A Brāhmaṇ of the third order who has passed through the stages of student and householder and has left his house and family for the forest (see āśram); a hermit, an anchorite; — the tree Bassia latifolia; — the palās-tree, Butea frondosa.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit -वान् (-vān), masculine nominative singular of -वत् (-vat).

def: s.m. = S ونانتر वनान्तर vanāntar, s.m. See s.v. ون van.
s.m. = S ونانت वनान्त vanānt, s.m. See s.v. ون van.
s.m. (f. -ī), A monkey; an ape.
s.f. A female monkey; — cowach, Carpopogon pruriens.

ety: vanas, prob. a form of the gen. of vana
def: s.m. 'Lord of the forest,' a large forest-tree; — a large tree bearing fruit, but apparently having no blossoms (as several species of the fig, the jack-tree, &c.); — a tree (generally); — an ascetic, a hermit, an anchorite.
s.m. = S وناشري वनाश्रय vanāśray, adj. See s.v. ون van.
adj. = S وناشرم वनाश्रम vanāśram, s.m. See s.v. ون van.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Deceiving, cheating; fraudulent, dishonest, deceitful, crafty; — a deceiver, rogue, cheat, knave, a vile fellow; — a jackal; — a musk-rat; — a tame ichneumon.
s.m. = S ونچنا वंचना vaṅćanā, s.f. The act of deceiving, cheating, defrauding; deception, deceit, fraud, knavery; — illusion, delusion, hallucination.
s.f. = S ونچن वंचन vaṅćan, s.m. The act of deceiving, cheating, defrauding; deception, deceit, fraud, knavery; — illusion, delusion, hallucination.
s.f. Wish, desire. (This word is of com. occurrence in Hindī as a suffix in the form ās, or āsā (for S. वाञ्छकः), e.g. miṭhās; niṅdāsā.)
s.m. Wishing, desiring.
adj. (f. -inī), Wishing, wishful, desirous; lustful.
s.f. A libidinous woman; a wanton.
part. (f. -ā), Wished, desired, longed for.
part. (f. -ā), Deceived, tricked, cheated, defrauded, deluded, imposed upon.

adj. (f. -ī) = vaṅćak, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Deceiving, cheating; fraudulent, dishonest, deceitful, crafty; — a deceiver, rogue, cheat, knave, a vile fellow; — a jackal; — a musk-rat; — a tame ichneumon.
suff., used to form possessives, i.q. want, and vān, qq.v. (cf. mand).

adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Maimed; defective; crippled; — tailless, docked; — impotent, emasculated; — one who has lost his hands and feet; one whose hands have been cut off; — a tailless animal; — a man who is circumcised, or who has no prepuce.

ety: S. वन्दकः; — also S. वन्दा, f.
def: s.m. = bandā, 'mistletoe,' q.v. s.m. Name of a place in Bundelkhand.
s.f. An unchaste woman.
s.m. The act of praising; praise, reverence, adoration, worship; salutation, homage, obeisance (to a Brāhmaṇ or superior by touching the feet, &c.); — flattery; — the face, mouth (i.q. vadan).
s.f. Praising; praise, worship, adoration; prayer.
s.f. Making obeisance, adoring; reverence, worship, adoration; — begging, soliciting, asking (for); — a drug for reviving the dead.
part. adj. (f. -ā), To be praised, or worshipped, or adored; praiseworthy; adorable; laudable, commendable, estimable; — to be saluted or made obeisance to.
s.m. corr. of bandhī = bandī, 'prisoner, captive.'

s.m. One who praises or extols, a praiser, panegyrist, encomiast; a flatterer; a bard, herald (whose business it is to proclaim the titles of a great man as he passes along, or who sings the praises of a prince in his presence, or accompanies an army to chaunt martial songs).
s.m. A prisoner, &c. (i.q. bandī, q.v.). s.f. A female slave, bondmaid (syn. launḍī)
subs: bāṅdī-kā beṭā or janā, s.m. lit. 'Son of a bondmaid'; an obedient or submissive person.

def: part. (f. -ā), Praised, extolled, adored, celebrated.
part. adj. (f. -ā), To be praised, praiseworthy, commendable, &c. (i.q. vandanīya, q.v.).
adj. (f. -ī) = banelā, q.v. (and cf. vanya). adj. Pertaining to the forest; sylvan, wild, savage.
adj. (f. -ī), Relating or belonging to a wood, or to water; — s.m. The fragrant grass Cyperus rotundus.
s.m. = بنگ baṅg, 'tin,' &c. q.v. s.f. Voice, sound, noise, cry, shout; the call to prayer by the muʼaẕẕin from the minarets or towers of the mosques (syn. aẕān); crowing (of a cock)
subs: v.n. To call Musalmāns to prayer; to crow (a cock)
s.f. The morning call to prayer
v.n. To cry out; to call
s.f. The call to prayer ( = bāṅg).

def: s.f. A bangle, &c. (i.q. baṅgrī, q.v.). s.f. A bracelet or 'bangle' made of glass or lac (also written baṅglī, vaṅglī; — ćūrī).

adj. & s.m. corr. of وانمي vān-may, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Consisting of words; relating to speech; endowed with speech; — eloquent, rhetorical; — loquacious; — s.m. Speech, lauguage; eloquence, rhetoric.
adv. = wahāṅ, q.v.; — s.f. = bāṅh, q.v.

s.m., corr. of vahnī, q.v. s.m. Fire; an epithet of Agni, the god of fire; — the fire of the stomach, the digestive faculty, gastric fluid; — digestion; appetite; — leadwort, Plumbago zeylanica; — the marking-nut plant; — a symbolical expression for the number three.

s.f. A small forest or wood (i.q. banī, q.v.). s.f. Pār kaheṅ ut vār hai, aur vār kaheṅ ut pār, Isī kināre baiṭh raho; yehī vār, yehī pār! The other bank calls this the other side, and this bank that, Sit quiet where you are; that side is this, and this side that. (Objects appear but as the point of view, each from the mind just takes its hue). vār khālī jānā , v. n. To miss; fall short; fail. this side. vār k. , v. a. 1. To charge; assault. 2. To make delay. Mat kar vār jo bhugte kār . Rus. Prov. Make no delay if success you'd essay. vār milnā , v. n. 1. To have one's turn. 2. To have leisure.

(f. -ī), added to subst. to derive nouns implying possession or relation generally; — e.g. go-wāl, or go-wālā, s.m. cow-keeper, cow-herd (fr. go, 'cow'); — ghaṭ-wāl, s.m. wharf-keeper, wharfinger (fr. ghāṭ, 'landing-place'); — nāʼo-wālā, s.m. boat-keeper, boatman (fr. nāʼo, 'boat'); — ghar-wālā, s.m. master or owner of the house (fr. ghar, 'house'); — kapṛā-wālā, s.m. cloth-man, cloth-merchant (fr. kapṛā, 'cloth'); — roṭī-wālā, s.m. bread-man, baker (fr. roṭī, 'bread'); — dillī-wālā, belonging to, or a native of, Delhi; — it is even suffixed to foreign words, e.g. bakas-wālā, box-man, pedlar (fr. bakas, 'a box'); — it is sometimes used to form adj. fr. subst., e.g. kifāyat-wālā, 'economical' (fr. kifāyat, 'economy'); — gunjāʼish-wālā, 'capacious, spacious, roomy' (fr. gunjāʼish, 'capacity')
subs: (a)ne-wālā (or ani-hārā) is added to the roots of verbs to form fut. act. part., or nouns of agency, e.g. paṛh-ne-wālā, 'one who is going (or is about) to read'; 'a reader'; — de-ne-wālā, 'who is about to give'; 'a giver'; — ho-ne-wālā, 'that is about to happen'; 'that has yet to come to pass'; 'which is yet possible.'

def: part. adj. Wandered, gone astray; lost; — one who has wandered, &c.
more: (part. pass. of وارفتن) adj. Wandered, lost. (Pers. plur. وارفتگان).

def: s.m. pl. (of next), Those who have gone astray; the lost.

s.m. A boar, wild boar, hog; — name of Vishṇu in the third or boar-incarnation (in which he raised the earth from the bottom of the sea with his tusks); — an array of troops in the form of a boar; — name of one of the eighteen lesser divisions (dvīp) of the world; — a particular measure; — name of a sacred place.
adj. Belonging to a boar, boarish; — s.m. (f. -ī), A boar
subs: s.m. Name of a place of pilgrimage on the river Gomtī (about thirty-two miles south of Ayodhyānāth), celebrated as the birth-place of Vishṇu under the form of a boar (see avatār).

def: s.f. A sow; — name of one of the Mātr̤is or divine mothers attending on Skand; — the earth; — a measure; — a species of esculent root or yam.

adj. & s.m. Restraining, checking, preventing, hindering, obstructing, opposing, resisting, thwarting; — anything which restrains, or obstructs, &c.; an obstacle; — an opponent; — a horse; — one of a horse's paces; — seat (of pain); — a sort of fragrant grass.
adj. (f. -ā), Pitiable, miserable, unhappy, poor, wretched, low, vile; — impure; — s.m. A sinner, &c.; — a name of Śiva; — battle, war.
s.m. Leaf (of a tree, or of a book, or of silver or gold, &c.); silver-leaf, gold-leaf; page (of a book); — a slice; — a card
more: n. m. 1. A slice. 2. A card. 3. Silver or gold leaf. 4. See pannā , 1, 2. varaq utārnā, yā kāḷnā , v. a. To slice; cut off a slice. varaq bikharnā , 1. To be scattered (the leaves of a book). 2. To become very thin. 3. To lose one's self-possession. varaq-sāz , One who makes gold or silver leaves. varaq kūṭnā , v. a. To hammer out gold or silver leaves. varaq-gardānī , n. f. Turning over the leaves of a book merely. varaq moṛnā , v. a. To turn over the corner of a page.

s. m. 1. A leaf (of a book, &c.). 2. A card, a slice. ورق اتارنا waraḳ utārnā, To page and line (a book). ورق تراشنا waraḳ tarāshnā, To cut cards. ورق الخيال waraḳu-l-ḵẖiyāl, s. m. Name of an intoxicating drug (Cannabis sativa). ورق داغي waraḳ dāg̠ẖ-ī, Written paper. ورق داغي کرنا waraḳ dāg̠ẖī karnā, To page (a book).
subs: (-kā), To slice, cut off a slice; — to page and line (a book)
(-ke), The leaves (of a book, &c.) to be scattered; — to become very thin; — to lose (one's) self-possession
(-kā), To cut a slice off (from); — to cut cards
s.m. A kind of intoxicating drug, Cannabis sativa (syn. bhang; sabzī)
s.m. The number of the page (of a book)
s.m. One who makes gold or silver leaves
(-kā), To cut off a slice (of), to slice
To beat out gold or silver leaf
s.f. Turning over the leaves (of a book)
(-kā), To turn down a leaf or page (of); to turn over the corner of a page.

ety: for A. ورقة waraqat, n. of un. fr. waraq
def: s.m. A leaf; — a paper; letter, billet; schedule.
adj. Of or relating to a leaf; leaf-like; — foliated; — flaky.
s.m. A wasp; hornet (i.q. varaṭ).
s.f. A wasp, &c. (i.q. varal; cf. burlā); — a goose (i.q. varaṭā).
